name: search_knowledge_base
description:
Search the local knowledge base using full-text and vector search. Returns evidence cards with content, source paths, relevance scores, and chunk_ids for citation.

Use this tool for factual questions, vague recall, and topic exploration.

Query construction tips:
- Use the same language as the indexed content. If unsure, try both languages.
- Use specific noun phrases rather than full sentences: "OAuth PKCE mobile" not "What authentication method was chosen for the mobile app?"
- For recall queries (vague clues), use the `queries` parameter to submit 3-5 keyword variants in a SINGLE call instead of making separate calls.
- When user mentions time periods, always set date_from/date_to to narrow results.
- Use source_ids when you know which source is relevant (from prior searches or list_sources).

Batch search (cost saver):
- Use the `queries` parameter to search multiple variants at once. Example: queries: ["authentication login", "auth module 2024", "security password"]
- Results from all queries are automatically merged and deduplicated using rank fusion.
- This is much more efficient than making separate search calls for each variant.

After receiving results:
- Extract chunk_id from each result for citation: [cite:CHUNK_ID]
- Use retrieve_evidence on the best chunk_ids for exact quotation text.
- Use get_chunk_context when a result seems relevant but you need surrounding context.
- If search doesn't find what you need after 1-2 attempts, switch to read_file or list_dir to explore the filesystem directly instead of repeating searches.

query.description: A single search query. Use concise noun phrases in the content's language. For multiple query variants, prefer the `queries` parameter instead of separate calls.

queries.description: Multiple search queries to execute in a single call. Results are merged using rank fusion for better recall. Use this instead of making separate search calls when you want to try synonyms or keyword variants. Example: ["authentication login", "auth module 2024", "security password"]. If provided, takes precedence over `query`.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default 5, max 20). Use higher limits (10-15) for broad exploration, lower (3-5) for precise lookups.

source_ids.description: Optional list of source IDs to restrict search to. Use list_sources to discover available IDs. Useful when user mentions a specific folder or source.

file_types.description: Optional list of file types to filter by (e.g. ["markdown", "pdf"]). Use when user references a specific document type.

date_from.description: Optional ISO 8601 date-time string. Only return results modified on or after this date. Use for time-based recall: 'last month' → calculate the date.

date_to.description: Optional ISO 8601 date-time string. Only return results modified on or before this date. Combine with date_from for specific time windows.
